preamble: Monophthongization of the diphthong [ui] to [i] typically involves the loss of the initial labial element, often resulting in a high front vowel.

examples.note: Loss of the rounded onset in the [ui] diphthong, common in various vowel reduction processes.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
